capture.cameraPose: desktop preview with right-side operator panel scrolled to live Quest evidence freshness fields
capture.captureCommand: Chrome DevTools MCP take_screenshot after local Vite navigation and frame-freshness instrumentation

notes: The ED chest-pain intent and EHR text remain visible, but the desktop preview still uses placeholder actors, sparse equipment, and a simplified bay rather than production clinical realism.
The visible patient is a primitive capsule/head actor with no skin, clothing, rigging, pain expression, or realistic hand representation; this does not close Patrick's headset realism finding.
The operator panel and Quest Evidence fields are readable in desktop preview, including frames, loop freshness, input, movement, trace, validation, and copyable JSON.

The screenshot verifies the evidence UI surface and live freshness text only; it does not prove immersive entry, hand-gesture locomotion, controller select latency, or Quest Browser clipboard behavior.
The in-scene actor and panels overlap in the desktop camera pose, and portions of the clinical/input panels are clipped by the preview framing; headset camera-pose checks remain required.
This artifact supports adversarial visual review of the desktop evidence panel and frame-freshness instrumentation. It is not physical Quest foreground frame pacing or headset readability evidence.
